mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods found in this batch. This batch contains helpers for Resque jobs, shipment tracking, and plan updates, but no printer enrollment or subscription setup methods. Enrollment methods likely exist in other batches containing subscription or printer management helpers.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription state verification methods found in this batch. UpdatePlanHelper.doesnt_see_free_months exists but it verifies UI display of free months, not the actual subscription state. Backend subscription verification methods likely exist in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification or setup methods found in this batch. Methods to check or set subscription status likely exist in other batches containing subscription management or API helper modules.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: No pause plan methods found in this batch. UpdatePlanHelper.cancellation_subscription exists for cancellation flow but not for pause plan flow. Pause plan methods likely exist in other batches containing instant ink dashboard or plan management page objects.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking a navigation link with the provided page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the page_name parameter. The XPath pattern is '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]'. The normalize-space function removes leading and trailing whitespace from the link text for more reliable matching. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is commonly used to navigate through the application's main navigation menu.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 require navigating to the Print and Payment History page. This generic navigation method could be used if 'Print and Payment History' is accessible via a nav-link in the main navigation menu. However, the actual navigation pattern for this specific page may differ and a dedicated page object method may exist in other batches.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will throw a TimeoutError. This is commonly used after navigation to ensure the target page has fully loaded before proceeding with further interactions.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 1 and 4 expected results require verifying that the Print and Payment History page loads successfully. This wait method could be used to ensure page load completion. However, the actual page title pattern may differ from 'Details for {page_title}' format, so this method may need adaptation or a different page load verification method may exist in other batches.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then calls inner_text() on the field element to extract all visible text content including nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is commonly used to extract data values from labeled fields on detail pages for validation in test assertions.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 8, 11, 14, 18, and 19 require extracting text values from labeled sections (complimentary pages value, additional pages value, total pages printed). This method demonstrates a pattern for extracting text from titled card sections which is similar to the billing cycle card structure. However, the actual billing cycle card may use different HTML structure and dedicated getter methods likely exist in other batches containing the PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Implements dynamic attribute access to automatically convert selector strings into Playwright locator objects. When an attribute is accessed on the page object (e.g., page_obj.login_button), checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If found, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name) and returns self.page.locator(locator_str) which creates and returns a Playwright Locator object for that selector. If the attribute name is not found in elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name. This magic method enables page objects to access locators as simple attributes without manually creating locator objects, making page object code much cleaner and more readable.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This is the base page object architecture that any PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object would extend. It provides the foundational locator management pattern used throughout the framework. While not directly used in test steps, it is the infrastructure that enables page objects to define and access locators cleanly. The actual PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object likely exists in other batches and would extend this base class.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with Resque job scheduler, managing background job execution, navigating application pages and menus, extracting data from page elements by title, accessing links within titled sections, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. This helper class encapsulates common patterns for Resque job orchestration and page element interaction that are reused across multiple test scenarios. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.
matched_helpers.usage_example:
RABaseHelper.access_page(page, 'Print and Payment History')
RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load(page, 'Print and Payment History')
text = RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title(page, 'Complimentary pages')
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper provides generic navigation and data extraction patterns that could be adapted for the billing cycle test case. The access_page method could be used for navigation, wait_page_to_load for page load verification, and get_field_text_by_title demonstrates a pattern for extracting text from titled sections similar to the billing cycle card structure. However, these are generic patterns and dedicated methods for the specific billing cycle card elements likely exist in other batches.

test_case_mappings.batch_analysis_summary: This batch contains helper classes for Resque job management (RABaseHelper), shipment tracking (ShipmentTrackingHelper), plan updates and cancellation (UpdatePlanHelper), and the base page object architecture (BasePageObject). None of these are directly relevant to the HP Instant Ink billing cycle period card test case requirements. The test case requires: (1) PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object with billing cycle card locators and methods, (2) Time manipulation utilities for 32-day shift, (3) Print job simulation API helpers, (4) Progress bar verification methods with color checking, (5) Tooltip interaction methods with device-specific logic, (6) Visual regression testing utilities, (7) Responsive testing utilities. None of these specific requirements are met by this batch. However, some generic patterns were found: RABaseHelper.access_page provides a navigation pattern, RABaseHelper.wait_page_to_load provides a page load wait pattern, RABaseHelper.get_field_text_by_title demonstrates a pattern for extracting text from titled sections (similar to billing cycle card structure), and BasePageObject provides the foundational locator management architecture that any page object would extend. These are architectural patterns rather than direct matches. The actual PrintAndPaymentHistoryPage page object, time manipulation helpers, print simulation helpers, progress bar verification methods, tooltip interaction methods, and visual/responsive testing utilities likely exist in other batches containing instant ink page objects, test utilities, or visual testing modules.
